test_name: Yajnadevam Sanskrit vs Parpola Dravidian falsification (E7 head-to-head)
outcome: PARPOLA EDGE: Both maps significant but Dravidian scores higher (24.5 vs 17.5). Both hypotheses receive non-trivial iconographic-anchor support; Parpola's edge suggests Dravidian-specific homonymies (miin = fish/star, vaTa = banyan/north, piLLai = squirrel/child) ARE doing real work.
verdict: T3 falsification: Parpola Dravidian map observed anchor score = 24.5 (p=0.0001); Yajnadevam Sanskrit map observed = 17.5 (p=0.0001). Score difference = +7.00 in favor of Parpola. PARPOLA EDGE: Both maps significant but Dravidian scores higher (24.5 vs 17.5). Both hypotheses receive non-trivial iconographic-anchor support; Parpola's edge suggests Dravidian-specific homonymies (miin = fish/star, vaTa = banyan/north, piLLai = squirrel/child) ARE doing real work.
